cardChroniclesCodes.title: Card Chronicles Codes
cardChroniclesCodes.subtitle: Redeem active Card Chronicles codes for Trait Rerolls, luck boosts, roll speed boosts, battle speed boosts, and progression potions.
cardChroniclesCodes.intro: Use codes from the Settings menu by selecting the gear icon at the bottom-left of the screen, scrolling to the Codes section, entering the code exactly, and pressing Enter. Several rewards are built for faster rolling and stronger card builds, so spend Trait Rerolls on cards you plan to keep instead of burning them on early throwaway pulls.

items.bestFor: Players who have already passed the early rolling gate.

items.rewardSummary: x5 Luck Potions, x5 Battle Speed Potions, x5 Roll Speed Potions, x3 Border Chance Potions, x3 Super Luck Potions, x3 Boss Potions, x15 Trait Rerolls
items.bestFor: Boosting roll sessions and wave farming after release milestones.
Fixing traits on your strongest long-term cards.
Mid-game players who have already rolled enough to unlock gated rewards.

items.rewardSummary: x10 Luck Potions, x10 Battle Speed Potions, x10 Roll Speed Potions, x5 Border Chance Potions, x5 Super Luck Potions, x5 Boss Potions, x15 Trait Rerolls
items.bestFor: Stacking potion value before a longer grind session.

items.rewardSummary: x3 Luck Potions, x3 Battle Speed Potions, x3 Roll Speed Potions, x1 Border Chance Potion, x1 Super Luck Potion, x5 Trait Rerolls
items.bestFor: New players starting their first real rolling and upgrade push.

cardChroniclesBeginnerGuide.title: Card Chronicles Beginner Guide
cardChroniclesBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the early loop: roll cards, build a lineup, upgrade useful pulls, and push through waves without wasting your best rewards.
cardChroniclesBeginnerGuide.intro: Card Chronicles is built around rolling anime-style cards, using them in fights, upgrading the cards that carry your lineup, and climbing through harder wave-based battles. The early goal is to form a working lineup and grow the cards that consistently win fights.

items.heading: Start by rolling for usable cards
items.description: Roll to build your first card pool before spending heavily. Your first job is to get enough options to form a working lineup, not to chase a perfect rare card immediately.
items.mistakeToAvoid: Do not spend premium reroll resources on the first low-value card that looks shiny.

items.heading: Build a small fighting lineup
items.description: Place your strongest available cards into a focused lineup for wave clearing. Prioritize cards that actually help you win fights over cards that only look rare in the collection screen.
items.mistakeToAvoid: Do not spread upgrades across every new pull.

items.heading: Upgrade cards that keep winning fights
items.description: Upgrade the cards that clear waves consistently and help you beat stronger foes. Card upgrades matter because the game loop includes unlocking stronger abilities and increasing battle value over time.
items.mistakeToAvoid: Do not abandon a useful upgraded card just because a fresh pull has higher rarity but no investment yet.

items.heading: Use roll and luck potions during focused sessions
items.description: Luck Potions, Super Luck Potions, and Roll Speed Potions deliver the most value when you are actively rolling for progress. They are wasted when activated casually and then left idle while you are distracted.
items.mistakeToAvoid: Do not activate multiple grind boosts unless you are ready to use the full window.

items.heading: Save Trait Rerolls for keeper cards
items.description: Trait Rerolls are one of the strongest code rewards because they improve cards you plan to keep. Spend them after you identify a card that belongs in your lineup for the long run.
items.mistakeToAvoid: Do not chase perfect traits before your lineup has stable cards.

items.heading: Push waves, then improve the weak slot
items.description: After each wall, identify the weakest card in your lineup and replace or upgrade that slot first. This keeps progression simple and avoids cluttering your inventory with half-upgraded cards.
items.mistakeToAvoid: Do not upgrade randomly after every battle.

cardChroniclesTierList.title: Card Chronicles Tier List
cardChroniclesTierList.subtitle: A progression-focused Card Chronicles tier list built around wave clearing, ability value, upgrade payoff, and long-term lineup usefulness.
cardChroniclesTierList.intro: Because Card Chronicles is a rolling game, the best early tier list is based on what a card does for your account, not just how rare it looks. Prioritize cards that clear waves, beat strong foes, and stay useful after upgrades before you spend resources trying to make a weak card feel special.

items.label: Main Carry Cards
cards: High-rarity cards with strong damage abilities
Cards that clear multiple enemies efficiently
Cards worth Trait Rerolls after upgrades
items.notes: Build around these cards first because they carry both battle progress and future reroll value.

items.label: Consistent Progression Cards
cards: Upgraded cards with consistent battle output
Cards that stay useful across several wave walls
Strong cards without perfect traits yet
items.notes: These cards are worth keeping until a clear S-tier replacement appears.

items.label: Specialist Cards
cards: Single-target damage cards for stronger foes
Cards that need more upgrades before becoming stable
items.notes: Use them when they solve a real battle problem, not because they look exciting for three seconds.

items.label: Starter and Placeholder Cards
cards: Unupgraded cards with weak ability value
Cards used only because the lineup has empty slots
items.notes: Replace these quickly and avoid spending Trait Rerolls on them.

cardChroniclesRollingRarity.title: Card Chronicles Rolling and Rarity Guide
cardChroniclesRollingRarity.subtitle: Understand how rolling, rarity, upgrades, potions, and Trait Rerolls fit together before turning your resources into wasted smoke.
cardChroniclesRollingRarity.intro: Rolling is the center of Card Chronicles progression: you roll to find better cards, use the best pulls in battle, upgrade useful cards, and spend boost items when they improve real progress. Rarity matters, but an upgraded card that clears waves is more useful than a fresh rare pull that has not been built yet.

items.whatItMeans: The main way to expand your card collection and search for stronger cards.
items.bestUse: Roll in focused sessions, especially when using Luck or Roll Speed Potions.
items.resourceNote: More roll volume improves your chance of finding a useful lineup upgrade.

items.whatItMeans: Rarer cards are the main chase target and carry stronger long-term value.
items.bestUse: Compare rarity with actual battle impact before replacing an upgraded card.
items.resourceNote: Do not assume a fresh rare card is better until it helps clear waves or strong foes.

items.whatItMeans: Upgrades strengthen useful cards and help unlock stronger combat value.
items.bestUse: Upgrade the cards that already win fights instead of every new card you pull.
items.resourceNote: Focused upgrades create faster progression than spreading resources thin.

items.whatItMeans: Card abilities are a major reason one card outperforms another in battle.
items.bestUse: Favor abilities that improve wave clearing, boss pressure, or lineup stability.
items.resourceNote: Ability value should guide upgrade priority more than collection appearance.

items.whatItMeans: Trait Rerolls improve cards you plan to keep in your main lineup.
items.bestUse: Spend them on high-value upgraded cards, not early placeholders.
items.resourceNote: Code rewards make Trait Rerolls easier to get, but they are still best saved for keeper cards.

items.system: Luck Potions and Super Luck Potions
items.whatItMeans: Boost items used to improve rolling sessions.
items.bestUse: Use them when actively rolling for upgrades or rare pulls.
items.resourceNote: Stack their value with planned playtime instead of activating them casually.

items.whatItMeans: Boost items that help increase rolling throughput.
items.bestUse: Use during grind windows where you want more rolls in less time.

items.system: Battle Speed Potions and Boss Potions
items.whatItMeans: Boost items aimed at faster combat and stronger progression attempts.
items.bestUse: Use while farming waves, pushing walls, or preparing for stronger foes.
items.resourceNote: These boosts deliver the most value when your lineup is already stable enough to win fights.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Card Chronicles

questions.question: What is Card Chronicles?
questions.answer: Card Chronicles is a Roblox anime card RNG battler where you roll cards, upgrade their abilities, build a lineup, and fight endless waves of strong card enemies.

questions.question: How do I redeem Card Chronicles codes?
questions.answer: Open the Settings menu from the gear icon at the bottom-left of the screen, scroll to the Codes section, type the code exactly, and press Enter to claim your Card Chronicles rewards.

questions.question: Which Card Chronicles codes should I use first?
questions.answer: Start with Card Chronicles codes that grant Trait Rerolls and potion bundles, then save rerolls for the strong keeper cards you plan to upgrade for your long-term lineup.

questions.question: How does rolling work in Card Chronicles?
questions.answer: Rolling is the core of Card Chronicles progression: you roll to expand your card collection, use the strongest pulls in battle, upgrade useful cards, and spend boost items during focused rolling sessions.

questions.question: Which Card Chronicles cards should I upgrade first?
questions.answer: Upgrade the Card Chronicles cards that consistently clear waves and beat strong foes before spending resources on every new pull, then apply Trait Rerolls to your keeper cards.

questions.question: Is Card Chronicles free to play on Roblox?
questions.answer: Yes, Card Chronicles is free to play on Roblox, with code rewards like potions and Trait Rerolls that speed up your rolling, upgrading, and wave progression.
